sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: AppLovin, 2분기 잉여현금흐름 7억 6,800만 달러, 앱스 사업을 트리플닷에 4억 달러에 매각·광고 플랫폼 집중

keyPoints.text: 6월 30일 앱스(Apps) 사업을 트리플닷 스튜디오(Tripledot Studios)에 현금 4억 달러와 지분으로 매각 완료했습니다.

businessChanges.text: 앱스 사업 매각으로 순수 광고·마케팅 플랫폼으로 사업 구조를 전환했습니다.
분기 말 클래스 A·B 보통주 발행주식 수는 3억 3,900만 주였습니다.

watchItems.text: 앱스 사업 매각 이후 순수 광고 플랫폼으로서 매출 성장 지속성이 핵심 관전 포인트입니다.
제시된 3분기 매출 가이던스 13억 2,000만~13억 4,000만 달러 달성 여부에 주목할 필요가 있습니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: on and Free Cash Flow was $768 million for the second quarter 2025. ● During the second quarter 2025, we repurchased and withheld 0.9 million shares of our Class A common stock, for a total cost of $341 million 1 . At the end of 2Q 2025, we had 339 million shares of our Class A and Class B common stock outstanding. ● On June 30, 2025 we completed the sale of our Apps business to Tripledot Studios for $400 million in cash, subject to closing adjustments, and equity consideration representing approximately 20% of Tripledot’s fully-diluted equity at the time of closing. No promissory note was issued as part of the tr  Normalized numeric aliases: $768 million = 7억 6,800만 달러; 0.9 million = 90만 달러; $341 million = 3억 4,100만 달러; 339 million = 3억 3,900만 달러; $400 million = 4억 달러; $768 = 768달러; $341 = 341달러; $400 = 400달러; $1,340 = 1,340달러; 1,070 million = 10억 7,000만 달러; $1,070 = 1,070달러; $1,090 = 1,090달러; 1,320 million = 13억 2,000만 달러; 1,340 million = 13억 4,000만 달러; 1,090 million = 10억 9,000만 달러.

TDA margin 81% 81% 1 Includes repurchased shares as well as withholdings upon net share settlement of vested equity awards. Total cost includes repurchase costs, including commissions and fees, as well as cash paid in connection with tax withholding and remittance obligations upon net share settlement 2 We have not provided the forward-looking GAAP equivalents for forward-looking non-GAAP metrics, specifically Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA margin, or a GAAP reconciliation as a result of the uncertainty regarding, and the potential variability of, reconciling items such as stock-based compensation expense. Accordingly, a reconciliation of these non-GAAP guidance metrics to their corresponding GAAP equivalents is not available without unreasonable effort. However, it is important to note that material changes to reconciling items could have a significant effect on future GAAP results
